finding_title: IAM Root Account Access Key Exists
analysis:
```json
{
  "mapping": {
    "NIST_SP_800_53_Rev_5": [
      "AC-2",
      "AC-3",
      "AC-6",
      "IA-2",
      "IA-5"
    ],
    "NIST_SP_800_171_Rev_2": [
      "3.1",
      "3.5"
    ],
    "HIPAA": [
      "164.308(a)(3)(ii)(B)",
      "164.312(a)(1)"
    ]
  },
  "business_risk": "The presence of active access keys for the root account poses a critical security risk, as it allows unrestricted access to all AWS services. This can lead to a full account compromise, resulting in unauthorized access to sensitive data, potential data breaches, and service disruptions. According to the IBM Data Breach Report 2025, the average cost of a data breach is $4.88 million, which can significantly impact the organization's financial standing, reputation, and customer trust. For a CISO, mitigating this risk is crucial to protect the organization's assets and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.",
  "remediation_code": "resource \"aws_iam_access_key\" \"root_access_key\" {\n  user = \"root\"\n\n  lifecycle {\n    prevent_destroy = false\n  }\n\n  provisioner \"local-exec\" {\n    when    = destroy\n    command = \"aws iam delete-access-key --access-key-id ${self.id} --user-name root\"\n  }\n}\n\nresource \"aws_iam_user\" \"admin_user\" {\n  name = \"admin_user\"\n}\n\nresource \"aws_iam_policy_attachment\" \"admin_policy_attachment\" {\n  name       = \"admin_policy_attachment\"\n  users      = [aws_iam_user.admin_user.name]\n  policy_arn = \"arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess\"\n}",
  "remediation_urgency": "IMMEDIATE"
}
```

finding_title: EC2 Security Group Allows SSH from Internet
CloudTrail Not Enabled in All Regions
MFA Not Enabled for IAM Users
Azure SQL Server TLS Version Outdated
Azure Key Vault Purge Protection Disabled
Azure NSG Allows RDP from Internet
Azure Activity Log No Alerts for Policy Changes
Azure Service Principal with Excessive Permissions
